title: Near-Oracle KV Selection via Pre-hoc Sparsity for Long-Context Inference
abstract: A core bottleneck in large language model (LLM) inference is the cost of attending over the ever-growing key-value (KV) cache. Although near-oracle top-k KV selection can preserve the quality of dense attention while sharply reducing computation and bandwidth, existing sparse methods generally rely on posterior heuristics. We propose Pre-hoc Sparsity (PrHS), which selects KV entries before attention scoring and provides explicit accuracy control. On LLaMA and Mistral families across GSM8K and CoQA, the approach reduces retrieval overhead by >90%, achieving 3× higher retrieval sparsity than the state-of-the-art HShare at matched or better accuracy.
